message:
feat(triggers): implement RAISE() action handling in trigger body statements

Add full RAISE() expression support inside trigger bodies, implementing
all four SQLite-compatible raise actions:

New types:
- TriggerRaiseDirective: captures parsed raise action (IGNORE/ABORT/FAIL/
  ROLLBACK) and optional error message from RAISE() in a trigger statement
- TriggerStatementOutcome: Continue or SkipDml (for RAISE(IGNORE) semantics)

New functions:
- trigger_statement_raise_directive(): inspects trigger body statements to
  detect bare SELECT RAISE(...) with optional WHERE predicate evaluation,
  matching SQLite's trigger syntax where WHERE guards conditionally fire
- apply_trigger_raise(): dispatches raise actions — IGNORE returns SkipDml
  to skip the current DML row, ABORT/FAIL return errors with the message,
  ROLLBACK performs transaction rollback then returns error
- execute_bound_trigger_statement(): wraps execute_statement with raise
  directive detection, applying the directive instead of executing normally

Updated fire_before_triggers() and fire_after_triggers() to route through
execute_bound_trigger_statement() and handle SkipDml outcome, replacing
the previous placeholder comment "No RAISE(IGNORE) for now".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
